behavior_summary: Manages a peasant NPC's interactions: cleans up per-NPC references when actors leave its fast trigger (U8P_PESANT1::leaveFastArea), responds to being looked at, used, or targeted by spells, and runs short and long conversational flows.
Prevents overlapping conversations globally via the somebodyTalking flag: use() checks/sets this flag to block simultaneous dialogs.

Dialog flows (PESANT1::0656 and PESANT1::030D) produce randomized greetings, present choice menus to the player (Item::ask), and orchestrate short waits/gestures (spawned helper methods) while restoring NPC activity when finished.
Cast reactions may call for help or spawn helpers/guards probabilistically and avoid action if the NPC is dead.
U8P_PESANT1::leaveFastArea removes stored references for the leaving actor (resetRef/FREE calls) and clears any process-exclusion/info bits so the NPC no longer points to that actor.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: each exported function is an event handler (look/cast/use/U8P_PESANT1::leaveFastArea) invoked by engine events — use() is the main player-interaction router and spawns dialog subroutines.
Use() examines the incoming frame (Item::getFrame) and the NPC map (Npc::getMap) to select which dialog routine to spawn (PESANT1::0656 or PESANT1::030D).

Dialog flow in PESANT1::030D loops on player choices returned by Item::ask, comparing response strings and branching to lines or exit; randomized choices (urandom) determine phrasing and whether to continue into the longer dialog.

Global synchronization: somebodyTalking is set to block other NPCs from starting conversations and cleared after refusal or completion; use() enforces this lock with short spawn/wait and bark sequences on contention.
Cleanup path: PESANT1::leaveFastArea is explicitly called on Event 10 (actor leaving fast area) and invokes resetRef/FREE operations for multiple stored slots to avoid stale references.
open_questions: Exact set of info/slot IDs that U8P_PESANT1::leaveFastArea clears and whether any are used outside dialog/cast handlers (slots are referred to but not enumerated in this summary).
Precise semantics of spawned METHOD::0BCF, METHOD::1090 and FREE::1C19 beyond inferred animation/delay roles — their side effects are inferred from usage patterns.
Whether any other global flags besides somebodyTalking are read or written by this script in unobserved code paths.
How map IDs map to specific dialog variants — selection logic is present but exact mapping (which map IDs produce which dialog) is partially inferred.

main_function.name: U8P_PESANT1::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: When an actor leaves the NPC's fast-area trigger, remove per-NPC references to that actor and clear related info/process exclusion so the NPC no longer holds stale links to the leaving actor.
entry_conditions: Event 10: triggered when an actor leaves the NPC's fast trigger region

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT1::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Cleanup handler for actors exiting the NPC's fast-area trigger. Calls resetRef/FREE to clear stored actor references and remove process exclusion.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT1::look
function_map.role: Respond to 'look' events by barking a short description; uses Npc::isDead to emit alternate corpse text.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT1::cast
function_map.role: Handle being targeted by spells/abilities: consults target state, may spawn helpers/guards or bark for help based on randomness and local flags; suppressed if NPC is dead.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT1::use
function_map.role: Primary player-interaction router. Detects talk attempts via Item::getFrame, enforces somebodyTalking lock, selects and spawns appropriate dialog routine (0656 or 030D) based on map/frame.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT1::030D
function_map.role: Long dialog subroutine implementing greeting, name-question loop via Item::ask, response branching, and restoring NPC activity; uses urandom for line variation.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT1::0656
function_map.role: Short greeting/identification routine that builds 'Why, I am <name>!' messages, offers a quick exchange, and can route into the longer dialog (030D) depending on responses/randomness.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawned as a short greeting dialog when map/frame indicate that variant of conversation should run.
Spawned as the longer interactive dialog when map/frame indicate that variant should run or when the short dialog routes into it.
Short greeting may transition into the longer dialog based on player choices/randomness.
Dialog routines interact in the flow; 030D may reuse short-lines or return control to activity restoration routines (inferred interaction).

external_calls.purpose: Clear per-NPC reference slots so the NPC no longer points at the leaving actor (primary cleanup operation).
Also used to free/clear stored info slots during cleanup (appears alongside resetRef usage).
Determine whether to use dead-corpse variant of bark text.
Emit a short descriptive string to the examining process (e.g., 'peasant <pid>' or 'dead peasant <pid>').
Check current NPC target to decide reaction to being cast upon; influences spawning of guards or pleas for help.
Randomly choose between different bark lines and whether to summon help or call for the town sorcerer.
Spawn helper processes (e.g., guard spawns or delay processes) as part of the reaction to spells.
Inspect the incoming use/frame token to detect a talk attempt or other interaction type.
Select map-specific dialog variants to spawn (0656 vs 030D).
Emit refusal/feedback messages when somebodyTalking prevents conversation or to speak lines during interactions.
Spawn dialog subroutines and short delay processes that coordinate waits/animations around speech.
Present choice menus to the player (e.g., 'What is your name?' with 'bye') and receive the selected string for branching.
Randomize greeting lines and response variations within the dialog loop.
Trigger small gesture/animation methods (METHOD::0BCF, METHOD::1090) and short delay process (FREE::1C19) around spoken lines.
Restore or change NPC activity/state after dialog completion.
Retrieve NPC display name to construct the identification line 'Why, I am <name>!'.
Randomly choose between alternate small-phrase variants during the short greeting.

writes.name: somebodyTalking
writes.likely_effect: Acts as a mutex to prevent multiple NPCs from starting blocking conversations at the same time; set when this NPC begins a conversation attempt and cleared when that attempt ends or is refused.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Manages a peasant NPC's interactions: cleans up per-NPC references when actors leave its fast trigger (U8P_PESANT1::leaveFastArea), responds to being looked at, used, or targeted by spells, and runs short and long conversational flows.
